preview_text:
The model identifies Turkey as a slight favourite, assigning them a 38% win probability compared to USA's 31%. This represents a modest gap; both teams occupy credible positions in the forecast, with neither dominant.

The draw probability of 31% ranks nearly equal to both outright outcomes, suggesting a competitive match where all three results remain plausible. The spread across W/D/L is relatively flat, indicating uncertainty. A stalemate remains as probable as either team securing three points, which points to a closely contested fixture.

The market consensus diverges noticeably from the model. Sportsbooks lean toward USA at 37%, a 6-percentage-point premium over the model's forecast, while pricing Turkey at 35%—roughly 3 points below the model. Polymarket prices track closer to the model on Turkey but also favor USA. The dashboard makes no claim about which estimate is correct; these gaps warrant attention from those building their own views.
